item.title: I talked to two people today about rent. — 151 replies — 604.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I talked to two people today about rent.

Open thread • Replies: 151 • Updated: 2026-06-17 18:31 UTC

OP

I talked to two people today about rent.

The first guy said he lives in Atlanta and pays about $2,300 a month in rent. I mentioned that works out to roughly $27,600 a year, which I personally think is a lot for rent. He also told me he was let go from a sales job at a small company about two weeks ago. Small company = low pay. When I commented on the rent being high, he responded by saying, “maybe it is a lot for you.”

I told him it is a terrible financial decision.
I also told him that I live in a major city and my monthly taxes alone are more than that.

Later, I spoke with another guy who pays around $2,000 a month for a luxury apartment. That’s about $24,000 a year. I didn’t ask about his income or job situation, but I shared that I thought the annual rent cost seemed very high.

I kept telling him his rent was a waste of money and he should buy a house. He told me he might not want to to live somewhere for so long. I asked him how he expected to build wealth. The conversation went back and forth with me saying it was a waste of “money down the drain.” He eventually admitted it was hard and bad use of money and insulted me with “life is hard when YOU don’t live in mommies basement.”
Wow.
He is literally throwing money down the drain and I trying to give me good advice.

It made me wonder if people commonly spend over $20,000–$30,000 a year on rent? And if so, how common is that relative to income and lifestyle choices?

Please look at picrel. Do people spend so much of their paycheque on rent? How does anyone become rich?

Curious what others think about this.

Early replies (first 3)

62372611
>>62372604
>I talked to two people today about rent.
no you didn't

62372621
>$24k per year rent is throwing money down the drain
>$50k per year interest payments for 30 years to "own" the same thing is building wealth

Lol

Lmao

62372624
>>62372604

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62376556
>>62372604
When you really think about it, home owners are losing a lot of money too. First there's the down payment, that money grows a lot faster in stocks. In some cases it may be worth more than the entire value of the house in 30 years. Housing simply can't compete with the gains from the stock market. Secondly, even if you own your home and it's a 600k asset. Living in it, is throwing money away. If you sold you could be making 60k a year from your investments. Renting for 30k a year would be a net gain.

Home owners are mostly idiots, unless they are landlords or rich and retired. It is the handicap that prevents high earners from ever making it.

62376833
>>62372604
It depends on the market of course but yeah $2300 is not high in most places these days. If you equate that to a house payment that is under $300k which in major metros is ghetto tier housing. There is nothing inherently wrong with renting from a financial standpoint, even Dave Ramsey would agree. If you are not planning to stay put for 5-10 years then you are better off renting. The transaction costs for home ownership are quite high, often 10% of the price. The most important factor is, as your picrel states, the percentage of your income you spend on housing. If you want to invest and get rich, stay under 25% of your take-home.

>>62378686
you just launched a whole interdimensional TED Talk powered by three screenshots, two podcasts, and a gas-station energy drink.
You're standing on a folding chair yelling, "EVERY SINGLE WOMAN CAN JUST BECOME RICH, DATE A CELEBRITY, FIND A SUGAR DADDY, OR MARRY SOME DEVOTED GUY," like life is a character-select screen in a video game where everybody spawns with max charisma and infinite social options.
My brother in chaos, that's not how reality works.
You took a handful of visible examples, put them in a blender, added internet rage, and came out with a theory that somehow explains half the human population. That's clown astronomy. You're looking at three bright stars and declaring you've mapped the entire galaxy.
And then when someone tells a story about a rude interaction on a dating app, your brain teleports straight back to the same speech. Like the conversation took a bus stop and you arrived riding a shopping cart with no wheels.
The wild part is that your argument assumes every person has equal opportunities, equal looks, equal luck, equal social skills, equal location, equal connections, and equal willingness to do the things you're listing. That's a level of simplification so aggressive it could flatten a mountain into a parking lot.
Reality is messy. Some people struggle. Some people succeed. Some get lucky. Some don't. Some are attractive and still miserable. Some are average and find great relationships. Human lives aren't assembled from four preset buttons.
So when you scream "EVERY SINGLE ONE," that's the clown horn giving itself CPR.
The issue isn't that you're making a point. The issue is that you're treating a giant, complicated reality like a meme template and acting surprised when people look confused.
You're not describing the world.
You're describing a cartoon version of the world that lives rent-free inside your own echo chamber.

item.title: Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology — 36 replies — 122.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-06-17 18:24 UTC

OP

This will be one of the greatest investment opportunities in our lifetime within another decade or so.

Tech is stagnating, just as every other industry is out of viable optimization methodologies. There is nothing new being created, every niche is saturated and optimized. AI will produce some value but it is ultimately a giant bubble that will never, and I mean never advance to the point of consciousness or intelligence (unless they use biological brains and do so correctly - they will fail). Science doesn't even know where consciousness comes.

That being said: sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness) will be the big new technology that solves real world problems. And the funny thing is: tech companies will eventually run out of other means of generating increasing revenue and will essentially HAVE to go this route, just like they're currently doing with AI. They have nothing else. This technology will have vast and continuous demand as it will entirely replace the old/current paradigm.

Early replies (first 3)

>>62375553

if it were viable, there would be news. most people who are lonely and horny want the sexbot. but porn and masturbators are the best we have. sexbots are an AI complete problem.

62378763
>>62375553
The whole "artificial womb will make women worthless" take sounds like peak clown-world panic, not reality. It assumes women only have value because biology currently gives them a monopoly on pregnancy. That's a bizarre reduction of women to a single biological function, as if their social, economic, intellectual, emotional, and political influence suddenly evaporates the moment technology changes the rules of the game.
In reality, if artificial wombs become viable, women are far more likely to leverage the technology than be displaced by it. History shows that when a burden is removed, people don't become less valuable—they gain more freedom to negotiate from a position of strength. Pregnancy carries physical risks, career interruptions, health complications, and enormous time costs. If technology can externalize part of that burden
The assumption that artificial wombs somehow "replace" women also misunderstands how power works. Technologies rarely erase human incentives; they rearrange them. Women would still be mothers, partners, decision-makers, professionals, innovators, and participants in shaping the social norms surrounding reproduction. The ability to separate reproduction from pregnancy could even increase female autonomy by allowing greater control over timing, health outcomes, and life planning.
The clownish part of the argument is imagining that society would wake up one morning and conclude that half the population has become obsolete because a medical device exists.
If anything, artificial wombs could become another tool that women strategically use to maximize freedom, reduce biological constraints, and negotiate family formation on terms that better align with their goals. The idea that it automatically diminishes women says more about a narrow view of female value than it does about the technology itself.

62378829
>>62378763
even more men troon out/fag out and use artificial wombs to justify mgtow. supply and demand.

62378845
>>62378763
While I semi agree with you, the whole “women become worthless” thing refers to how men have to approach women for sex and family. It effectively cuts the major reason men interact with women that are not their family.

62378879
>>62378845
the whole female liberation is kinda retarded. by breaking tradition, women enable fag meta. among zoomers like 30% are open fags. if tradition collapses, men will just bum other men. if we get actually good biotech and ability for the bottom men to look decent, guys will stop paying any attention to femoids at all. femoids just can't compete, no man actually genuinely wants to talk to them or be in their company. the only two reasons we're not 100% fags are reproduction(solved by incel bags) and majority bottom guys(like all of you anons) being really ugly. if you could have a family with a cute anon you'd do it and never even recall that there are women. greeks understood this.

62378914
>>62378829
Bro is running an entire civilization theory off Xbox lobby economics

"Supply and demand" lmao. You talk like women are a commodity market and men are consumers deciding between brands. Human relationships aren't a Steam sale.

The funniest part is the assumption that most men only interact with women because they're forced to for reproduction. If that were true, then every married couple would instantly stop caring about each other the second they had kids. Reality check: companionship, attraction, love, friendship, shared goals, and basic social connection exist. Touch grass.

And this "fag meta" theory is pure clown math. You're looking at some increase in social acceptance and then leaping straight to "all men will abandon women." By that logic, because pizza exists, everyone should stop eating burgers. People don't suddenly switch sexual orientation because biotech got better. That's not how any of this works.

The "women can't compete" line is especially hilarious. Compete at what? Being women? If men are attracted to women, then women aren't competing with men in the first place. You're treating attraction like it's a spreadsheet instead of a biological preference.

Also, if artificial wombs ever became widespread, they wouldn't only change incentives for men. Women would gain freedom from pregnancy risks too. The technology cuts both ways. Pretending it's some one-sided male victory is like declaring the invention of bicycles a victory over walking.

The entire argument boils down to: "tradition kept society together because men had no options." That's not a defense of tradition; that's an admission your system depends on people being trapped.

So instead of some giga-brain future where every dude becomes a biotech-enhanced bachelor living with the homies, the far more likely outcome is that people keep doing what they've always done: forming relationships with the people they're actually attracted to.

Clown forecast rejected

item.title: So I made 90k from MSTR on xmas in 2024. — 7 replies — 8.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
So I made 90k from MSTR on xmas in 2024.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-17 17:52 UTC

OP

So I made 90k from MSTR on xmas in 2024.
I have around 500k and im a jobless neet.
I have been waiting for a bottom to get back in, im considering a go-all-in suicide stack.

Im just pretty fucking sure this thing is going to teleport to $300 easily the moment BTC starts moving convincingly. The cope seeting and dilating will be next level as "saylor is getting liquidated"fags get exposed again as they realize they missed the bottom.

The question is, where is the bottom?

MSTR bottomed months before BTC did, as well as peaking months before BTC did. It sort of predicts the future.
If you look at what MSTR is doing. It bottomed at $100 in February. Meanwhile BTC did a new lower low recently.
MSTR did an almost 100% move on May when the market sniffed a potential bottom and reversal on BTC.

Read that again.

The stock is clearly not dead, it just depends on the expectations of BTC. The moment we get a new narrative where higher prices are expected, this thing flies.

I can see Trump coming up with something to get the crypto crowd back on the midterms. Saylor has connections with the Trumps. I can see the government having a stake on the company or potentially buying it and making it part of the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve.

Then add in the macro being helpful such as more money printing. Even if AI gets a lot of money now, they will print enough that BTC runs again, plus gravity is real and AI stocks will get overpriced and BTC will outperform again.

Case in point, I only need to ride a 3x on a high beta move with my entire portfolio so I can finally retire. Where im from 1.5 million is enough.
